'''Makali P. Aizue''' (born 1977) is a Papua New Guinean former professional  [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|pr}} for [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in [[RFL League 1|Kingstone Press League 1]].

==Personal life==
Aizue was born in [[Goroka]], [[Eastern Highlands Province]], Papua New Guinea.

== Club career ==
=== Goroka Lahanis===
Aizue started playing for the [[Goroka Lahanis]] from 1998 to 2001.

=== Hull Kingston Rovers ===
Aizue joined [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in 2004 after leaving the Lahanis. He played for 43 games over 4 years.

=== Halifax RLFC ===
Aizue went to Halifax in 2011 and became the first prop choice for the club. He left the club in September 2012.

=== Dewsbury Rams RLFC ===
Aizue joined [[Dewsbury Rams]] in 2013 till the end of 2015.

=== Doncaster RLFC ===
Aizue has signed for [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] for the 2016 season.

== Representative career ==

=== Papua New Guinea ===
Aizue was selected in the [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team#Current squad|PNG training squad]]<ref name="PNG name preliminary squad">{{cite web|url=http://www.rlwc08.com/news/news/archive/2008/08/03/png-name-preliminary-squad.aspx |title=PNG name preliminary squad |publisher=RLWC.com |date=2008-08-01 |accessdate=2008-08-06 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100410150913/http://www.rlwc08.com/news/news/archive/2008/08/03/png-name-preliminary-squad.aspx |archivedate=2010-04-10 }}</ref> and eventually for the [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team#Current squad|PNG squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Wilshere handed Kumuls captaincy">{{cite web|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7669326.stm|title=Wilshere handed Kumuls captaincy|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-14|accessdate=2008-10-14}}</ref><ref name="Rovers' prop forward granted bail over alleged beating.">{{cite news|url=http://www.accessmylibrary.com/coms2/summary_0286-13810185_ITM|title=Rovers' prop forward granted bail over alleged beating.|newspaper=Hull Daily Mail|date=2006-03-07|accessdate=2008-11-05}}</ref>

He played for Papua New Guinea in the [[2010 Four Nations]] tournament.
